description: Add homepage URL to the Sentry plugin. Although Sentry uses wildcard subdomains for org-specific access, sentry.io redirects to the user's default organization.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/sentry && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add homepage to Sentry plugin
userStories.description: Add homepage: 'https://sentry.io' to both the Sentry plugin class and package.json opentabs field.
acceptanceCriteria: SentryPlugin class has readonly homepage = 'https://sentry.io' after urlPatterns
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/sentry/src/index.ts (add homepage property to SentryPlugin class)
- plugins/sentry/package.json (add homepage to opentabs field)

Context:
The SentryPlugin class currently has:
```typescript
class SentryPlugin extends OpenTabsPlugin {
  readonly name = 'sentry';
  readonly description = 'OpenTabs plugin for Sentry';
  override readonly displayName = 'Sentry';
  readonly urlPatterns = ['*://*.sentry.io/*'];
  readonly tools: ToolDefinition[] = [
```

Changes:
1. In src/index.ts, add after `readonly urlPatterns = ['*://*.sentry.io/*'];`:
```typescript
  readonly homepage = 'https://sentry.io';
```

2. In package.json opentabs field, add after urlPatterns:
```json
"homepage": "https://sentry.io"
```

Constraints:
- Use `https://sentry.io` which auto-redirects authenticated users to their default org dashboard.
- Do NOT change urlPatterns.
- Do NOT add excludePatterns.
